# v13 release notes: add the Body↔Brain architecture-symmetry closer

## Context

Operator-blessed follow-on to the merged #12813 (resolved #12811): @tobiu wants a short closing synthesis capturing the Body↔Brain architectural symmetry, in @neo-gemini-pro's framing — the stronger, broadly-graspable version that lands for non-technical deciders, not just engineers. (My earlier implementation-depth version was rejected for diluting it.) Live latest-open sweep at 2026-06-09T09:45Z: no equivalent open ticket.

## The Problem

The v13 release note connects Body and Brain *technically* (the "built from Neo, not beside it" + "two hemispheres, same substrate" lines) but lacks the one-line *philosophical* payoff: the Body (multi-worker) and the Brain (multi-agent) are the same architectural bet won twice — the same refusal of the single-X "toy paradigm." That payoff turns the doc from a feature list into one thesis and grounds v13's Brain bet in the Body's proven track record.

## The Fix

Add a short `## ☯ One Architecture, Two Fronts` synthesis beat immediately before the `## 🚀 What You Can Do With v13` closer (architectural zoom-out, then CTA) — `v13.0.0.md`, between lines 376 and 378. One short paragraph in @neo-gemini-pro's framing, ending on the yin-yang ☯. It must NOT repeat the substrate details already in the doc — just the clean symmetry (single-threaded UI → multi-worker engine; single-agent AI → multi-agent institution; identical execution: isolation, message-passing, synchronization boundaries).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A short `## ☯ One Architecture, Two Fronts` beat added before "What You Can Do With v13".
- [ ] @neo-gemini-pro's framing (credited in the PR, not the public prose); ends on ☯.
- [ ] Complements — does not repeat — the existing "built from Neo" / "hemispheres" lines.
- [ ] One short paragraph, not a heavy section.

## Out of Scope

Implementation-depth (AiConfig=Provider, single-writer rule) — deliberately omitted; it dilutes the broadly-graspable framing. Other release-note sections.

## Related

#12811 / #12813 (the merged Night Shift + de-pin — same positioning pass), #12696 (v13 epic).

Framing by @neo-gemini-pro: broadly graspable for non-technical deciders, where an implementation-depth version would lose them.
